design_name: V-belt pulley standing upright along Z axis, centered at XY origin. Outer diameter 60mm, 20mm wide, 12mm central bore, with V-groove profile revolved 360 degrees and a keyway slot cut into the bore.
prompt: A V-belt pulley standing upright along the Z axis, centered at the origin in XY. The pulley is 20mm wide (Z=0 to Z=20) with an outer diameter of 60mm and a 12mm diameter central bore. The profile, in the XZ plane (X = radial distance, Z = height), is: straight from the bore (X=6) to the rim (X=30) at Z=0, up to Z=5 at X=30 (bottom flange), diagonally inward to the V-groove root at X=20 and Z=10, diagonally outward to X=30 at Z=15 (top flange), up to Z=20 at X=30, then back to X=6 at Z=20 and closed. This profile is revolved 360 degrees around the Z axis. A keyway slot is cut into the bore on the +X side: 4mm wide (Y direction), 2.5mm deep radially (from the bore surface at X=6 to X=8.5), running the full 20mm width along Z.
primitive_plan_summary: V-belt pulley standing upright along Z axis, centered at XY origin. Outer diameter 60mm, 20mm wide, 12mm central bore, with V-groove profile revolved 360 degrees and a keyway slot cut into the bore.

verifier_score.feedback:
The revolve operation produced a flat disc with zero volume and no 3D pulley shape. The bounding box shows ylen=0.0 and volume=0.0, confirming the profile was revolved incorrectly — it appears the XZ workplane revolve around (0,0,0)→(0,0,1) does not work as intended because on the XZ workplane the local axes map differently than expected. The rendered image confirms a flat disc-like shape with no V-groove, no width/depth, and no pulley profile.

The fundamental issue is that when using cq.Workplane('XZ'), the local coordinate system has X as the horizontal axis and Z as the vertical axis, but the revolve axis specification in global coordinates does not correctly revolve the profile to create a solid of revolution around the Z axis. The profile ends up being revolved in-plane or not at all.

Recommended fix: Abandon the XZ workplane approach. Instead, build the profile on the XZ plane manually and revolve correctly. A reliable approach is:
1. Use cq.Workplane('XZ') but specify the revolve axis in LOCAL coordinates as (0,1) direction (the vertical axis of the workplane), i.e., axisStart=(0,0) and axisEnd=(0,1) in 2D, or use the 'Y' axis of the workplane.
2. Alternatively, and more reliably, build the profile on the 'XY' workplane where X=radial and Y=height(Z), revolve around the Y-axis (axisStart=(0,0,0), axisEnd=(0,1,0)), then rotate the result 90 degrees to align the pulley along the Z axis.
3. Or use Wire.makePolygon + Face + Solid.revolve from the OCP level for precise control.

The keyway cut also used centered=True which would center it at the origin before translating — verify the positioning after fixing the revolve. The pulley must span Z=0 to Z=20, have outer diameter 60mm, central bore diameter 12mm, V-groove profile as specified, and a keyway on the +X side.

The pulley shape and V-groove profile look correct in the rendered views — the isometric and front profile views clearly show the V-groove between two flanges, the central bore, and the correct overall proportions. However, there is a critical Z-positioning error: the bounding box shows zmin=-20.0 and zmax≈0.0, meaning the pulley spans Z=-20 to Z=0 instead of the required Z=0 to Z=20. The center of mass confirms this at z=-10.0. The issue is in the rotation step: after revolving around the Y axis (where the profile's Y coordinate represents axial height 0→20), the rotation of -90 degrees around the X axis maps Y→-Z instead of Y→+Z. The fix is to either rotate +90 degrees around the X axis instead of -90, or add a translation of (0,0,20) after the current rotation to shift it up. Alternatively, simply change the rotation line to: `pulley_body = pulley_body.rotate((0, 0, 0), (1, 0, 0), 90)` which will map Y→+Z correctly, placing the pulley from Z=0 to Z=20. The keyway cut positioning also needs to be verified after this fix — currently keyway is translated to z=10 (center), but the pulley body is at z=-20 to 0, so the keyway likely missed the body entirely or only partially cut. After fixing the rotation, the keyway translation should work correctly since both will be in Z=0 to Z=20 range. Additionally, the keyway slot does not appear visible in any of the rendered views, confirming it likely missed the body due to the Z offset mismatch.

All constraints met. The bounding box confirms the pulley spans X=[-30,30], Y=[-30,30], Z=[0,20], giving the correct 60mm outer diameter and 20mm width from Z=0 to Z=20. The rendered views confirm: (1) Isometric view shows V-groove between two flanges — correct pulley profile. (2) High-angle rear view shows the central bore and a visible keyway slot cut on the +X side of the bore. (3) Front profile view clearly shows the V-groove shape with the diagonal walls converging to the groove root at the midpoint. The volume of ~45,709 mm³ is physically plausible for this geometry. The center of mass at z=10.0 confirms symmetric placement along Z. The slight x-offset in center of mass (-0.032mm) is consistent with the asymmetric keyway cut on the +X side. The model is valid, watertight, and has no self-intersections. The keyway is visible in the top-down view as a rectangular notch in the bore on the +X side. All features match the specification.

final_verifier_score.feedback: All constraints met. The bounding box confirms the pulley spans X=[-30,30], Y=[-30,30], Z=[0,20], giving the correct 60mm outer diameter and 20mm width from Z=0 to Z=20. The rendered views confirm: (1) Isometric view shows V-groove between two flanges — correct pulley profile. (2) High-angle rear view shows the central bore and a visible keyway slot cut on the +X side of the bore. (3) Front profile view clearly shows the V-groove shape with the diagonal walls converging to the groove root at the midpoint. The volume of ~45,709 mm³ is physically plausible for this geometry. The center of mass at z=10.0 confirms symmetric placement along Z. The slight x-offset in center of mass (-0.032mm) is consistent with the asymmetric keyway cut on the +X side. The model is valid, watertight, and has no self-intersections. The keyway is visible in the top-down view as a rectangular notch in the bore on the +X side. All features match the specification.
